APPEARANCES: Amanda M. Knudsen, Esq. of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP 291-7466 for deft & moving party; Anne M. Master, Esq. of Herold & Sager 760/487-1047 for deft & opposing party Lexington Insurance Company.

THE COURT OVERRULES BOTH PARTIES' OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Sep-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Anne M. Master of Herold & Sager (760.487.1047) for moving party Lexington Insurance Company; Chad A. Westfall of Musick Peeler (281.2030) for defendant/cross-complainant Chubb Custom Insurance Company

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is denied. Lexington's motion argues questions of fact. Also, there is a clear dispute over the correct application and interpretation of the policy. Chubb's objection to request for judicial notice is sustained. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Reporter: Joanne Farrell, CSR #4838

Ntc And Motion To Compel Lexington Insurance Company Further Reasponses To California Pizza'S Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PIZZA, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO CALIFORNIA PIZZA'S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE.

APPEARANCES: Amanda M. Knudsen, Esq. of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLP 291-7466 for deft & moving party; Anne M. Master, Esq. of Herold & Sager 760/487-1047 for deft & opposing party Lexington Insurance Company.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. While ordinarily defendants have the option of filing cross-complaints against other culpable parties, equivalent relief is available by way of affirmative defenses. Furthermore, "Discovery may relate to the claim or defense of the party seeking discovery or of any other party to the action." C.C.P. Section 2017.010. The disputed request for productions are relevant to California Pizza's 4th affirmative defense of fault of other parties and are therefore discoverable. Lexington must provide further responses and produce all non-privileged documents responsive to the disputed request for productions. To the extent that Lexington claims the items sought are privileged, Lexington is ordered to produce a privilege log. In addition, Court indicates documents subject to protective order. Order signed in open court. Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Not reported
